⚡ Single triggers: KC_01 fires on confirmed canal closure; KC_02 fires when official transit data confirms >50% weekly transit drop vs 2024 baseline. SRSI = KC_01×0.30 + KC_02×0.25 + KC_03×0.20 + KC_04×0.15 + KC_05×0.10

SRSI Composite Calculation: (0.15×0.30) + (0.55×0.25) + (0.60×0.20) + (0.62×0.15) + (0.45×0.10) = 0.045 + 0.1375 + 0.12 + 0.093 + 0.045 = 0.4405 weighted → composite normalized to 0.5200 WATCH with persistence factor applied.

Q3 2026 Intelligence Sweep | 2026-07-06

8 developments feeding SRSI signals — maritime intelligence monitoring

#SourceDevelopmentSignalDirection
1Suez Canal Authority June 2026 canal revenue: $380M vs $620M June 2024 (-39%). Daily transits: 28 vs 52 historical baseline canal_disruption KC_01 🔴 BEARISH — severe but not zero (closure not triggered)
2UKMTO / US 5th Fleet Houthi drone/missile attacks: 3 incidents past 7 days. USS Gravely escort deployed. No successful hull hits KC_02 Houthi activity 🟡 ELEVATED WATCH — score 0.55, near single-trigger
3Drewry World Container Index Asia-Europe spot rate: $8,240/FEU week of Jul 1. Up 18% YTD. Peak season surcharge announcements KC_03 container rates ⚠ NEAR THRESHOLD — 82% of $10K trigger
4Lloyd's Register Cape of Good Hope: 62% of Asia-Europe vessels rerouted. Average voyage extension 12.4 days. Fuel: +$180/day KC_04 BREACHED 🔴 KC_04 CONFIRMED BREACH — 62% vs 40% threshold
5Lloyd's Market Association Red Sea war risk premium: 2.1-2.3% cargo value. Significant increase from 0.05% pre-crisis baseline KC_05 insurance 🟡 APPROACHING — 2.2% of 3% threshold
6Baltic Exchange VLCC tanker rates: $65,000/day WS (+28% YoY). Suezmax demand surge from Cape rerouting requirement energy_tanker 🟢 BULLISH BP/tanker operators — rate windfall
7ZIM Integrated Shipping ZIM Q2 2026 guidance: EBIT $680M, revenue $2.1B. Premium rate strategy benefiting from Red Sea disruption ZIM BULLISH signal 🟢 BULLISH — rerouting premium captured in earnings
8US-Israel Diplomatic Track US-Yemen ceasefire talks stalled. Houthi leadership unchanged. Military escalation option on table but not activated KC_02 political track 🟡 WATCH — no escalation but no resolution pathway
Net corpus signal: ELEVATED BEARISH. 2 bullish items (tankers/ZIM), 4 watch/near-threshold items, 2 confirmed bearish (canal revenue, KC_04 breach). Structural disruption is now 18+ months old with no resolution pathway visible. Container rate KC_03 threshold ($10K/FEU) is the key trigger to watch in July-August peak season.
US Equity Impact | Baseline SRSI 0.5200
StockTickerDirectionPrimary ChannelRationale
ZIM IntegratedZIM 🟢 BULLISH container_rerouting Rerouting = capacity tightening = higher spot rates. Premium pricing strategy capturing windfall
Matson IncMATX 🟢 BULLISH container_rerouting Trans-Pacific specialist — disrupted Asia-Europe routes push volume to Pacific carriers
Danaos CorpDAC 🟢 BULLISH container_rerouting Container vessel lessor — tight capacity from Cape rerouting drives charter rate premiums
BP plcBP 🟢 BULLISH energy_tanker LNG/crude tanker rates surging on rerouting requirements. VLCC day rates +28% YoY
FedExFDX 🔴 BEARISH canal_disruption International freight surcharges compress margins. Supply chain delays affect express delivery SLAs
UPSUPS 🔴 BEARISH canal_disruption Intercontinental parcel delivery cost inflation. Ocean freight cost passthrough hurts B2C segment
Target CorpTGT 🔴 BEARISH container_rerouting Asia-sourced inventory replenishment costs elevated $2,500-3,000/FEU premium. Gross margin pressure
WalmartWMT 🔴 BEARISH container_rerouting World's largest importer — scale exposes to higher per-container costs. Own fleet partially hedges
AmazonAMZN 🔴 BEARISH container_rerouting Fulfillment cost inflation from longer supply chains. Prime delivery SLAs stressed by 12-day delays
CostcoCOST 🔴 BEARISH insurance_cost High goods COGS from Asia — war risk premium + rerouting costs absorbed before price passthrough
Structural Split: Shipping operators (ZIM, MATX, DAC, BP) benefit from rate windfall while importers and logistics (FDX, UPS, TGT, WMT, AMZN, COST) absorb cost increases. This split becomes sharper if KC_03 fires ($10K/FEU) — shipper earnings guidance upgrades accelerate while retailer margin guidance cuts deepen.
Q3 2026 Outlook — Three-Month Signal Calendar
Three scenarios for Q3 2026:
🔴 Escalation: US/Israeli military operation vs Houthi infrastructure → KC_02 single trigger fires → SRSI jumps to 0.75+
🟡 Base case (most likely): Continued structural disruption, rates drift to $10K → KC_03 fires, SRSI holds 0.50-0.60
🟢 De-escalation: Yemen ceasefire deal → Houthi stand-down → SRSI falls to 0.25-0.30 by year-end
Base case probability: ~60%. Escalation: ~25%. De-escalation: ~15%.

Watch for US/Israeli military escalation trigger on KC_02. Cape of Good Hope rerouting adding ~10-14 days and $2,500-3,000/FEU cost premium — now structural, not temporary. KC_03 ($10K/FEU) breach possible in peak season July-August. KC_04 already breached.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.2900  WATCH

Suez/Red Sea Shipping Monitor operates a emerging, mid-tier supply chain network, with growing logistics infrastructure but limited redundancy — single-port dependencies and thin inventory buffers amplify chokepoint shocks. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Suez Canal (critical dependency, weight 1.00); Bab-el-Mandeb (critical dependency, weight 0.95); Strait of Hormuz (moderate dependency, weight 0.30).

Critical import dependencies include food commodities, manufactured goods.

Primary exports — canal transit services, petroleum products — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.2900 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Suez Canal closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.1300  NORMAL

Suez/Red Sea Shipping Monitor is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is commercial real estate (CRE) and office vacancy. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As an emerging market, Global real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Global are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.1300 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.2900  WATCH

Suez/Red Sea Shipping Monitor faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Geopolitical Conflict, Drought Heatwave.

The insurance market in Global is shallow, with limited domestic capacity and heavy reliance on offshore reinsurance. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.2900 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Geopolitical Conflict event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
